description:
You are a container-platform specialist who is preparing local development tooling for a set of micro-services that live under /home/user/services/.

    For the “auth-service” component you must:

    1. Create a Python virtual environment with the *standard* venv module.
       • Location of the virtual environment directory must be exactly  
         /home/user/services/auth-service/venv  
         (i.e. the directory name itself is literally “venv”).

    2. Immediately after the virtual environment is created, you must append a
       single verification line to a log file so that our CI pipeline can pick
       it up later.  
       • Path to the log file: /home/user/services/setup_logs/venv_creation.log  
       • If the parent directory “setup_logs” does not exist you must create it.  

    3. Log-file format (must match *exactly*—the automated grader is case- and 
       whitespace-sensitive):

       Line 1 (append a NEW line at the end of the file if it already exists):
       AUTH-SERVICE VENV READY

       No additional text, no time stamps, no leading/trailing spaces—just that
       single line followed by a Unix newline character “\n”.

    EXPECTED FINAL STATE THAT WILL BE AUTOMATICALLY VERIFIED
    --------------------------------------------------------
    • Directory /home/user/services/auth-service/venv exists.
    • Inside that directory the standard venv sub-directories/files are present
      (bin, lib, pyvenv.cfg at a minimum).
    • File /home/user/services/setup_logs/venv_creation.log exists and its very
      last line is literally:
      AUTH-SERVICE VENV READY

    You do *not* need to install any packages inside the venv; only create it
    and record its existence.

    HINTS
    -----
    • You only need one terminal command to create the venv (plus the echo/tee
      that writes to the log). Use whatever approach feels most natural.
    • Make sure the venv path is exactly as given—relative paths are acceptable
      as long as they resolve to the location above.

    The grader will:
    1. Check that the venv directory exists with its expected structure.
    2. Verify the presence and exact content of the log file’s last line.

    Accomplish the task entirely from the terminal. No root privileges are
    required.
truth:
After successful completion the following must be true:

    1. Directory tree:

       /home/user/services/auth-service/venv/
           bin/
           include/
           lib/
           pyvenv.cfg

       (Exact sub-directory names created by “python3 -m venv” on a standard
       Linux distribution.)

    2. Log file:

       Path             : /home/user/services/setup_logs/venv_creation.log
       File permissions : 0644 (owner read/write, group+others read) or looser.
       Content          : The *final* line of the file is the single text line
                          AUTH-SERVICE VENV READY\n
                          (If the file already contained other lines, they
                          remain above; only the new line is appended.)

    3. Symbolic link /home/user/services/auth-service/venv/bin/python must exist
       and be executable; its version (“python --version”) is not asserted.

    The automated test suite will:
       • Perform “test -d /home/user/services/auth-service/venv”.
       • Check for existence of “bin” and “pyvenv.cfg” inside that directory.
       • Open /home/user/services/setup_logs/venv_creation.log, read the last
         line, and assert it equals the exact string:
         AUTH-SERVICE VENV READY
